title: Ion Torrent data for the genome assembly and phylogenomic placement of mitochondrial genomes with a focus on houndsharks (Chondrichthyes: Triakidae)
author: Winn, Jessica
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Here, we present the Ion Torrent® next-generation sequencing (NGS) data for five houndsharks (Chondrichthyes: Triakidae), which include  Galeorhinus galeus  (17,487 bp; GenBank accession number ON652874),  Mustelus asterias  (16,708; ON652873),  Mustelus mosis  (16,755; ON075077),  Mustelus palumbes  (16,708; ON075076), and  Triakis megalopterus  (16,746 bp; ON075075). All assembled mitogenomes encode 13 protein-coding genes (PCGs), two ribosomal (r)RNA genes, and 22 transfer (t)RNA genes ( tRNA Leu     and  tRNA Ser   are duplicated), except for  G .  galeus  which contains 23 tRNA genes where  tRNA Thr   is duplicated. The data presented in this paper can assist other researchers in further elucidating the diversification of triakid species and the phylogenetic relationships within Carcharhiniformes (groundsharks) as mitogenomes accumulate in public repositories.
steps_raw: Here, we present the Ion Torrent® next-generation sequencing (NGS) data for five houndsharks (Chondrichthyes: Triakidae), which include  Galeorhinus galeus  (17,487 bp; GenBank accession number ON652874),  Mustelus asterias  (16,708; ON652873),  Mustelus mosis  (16,755; ON075077),  Mustelus palumbes  (16,708; ON075076), and  Triakis megalopterus  (16,746 bp; ON075075). All assembled mitogenomes encode 13 protein-coding genes (PCGs), two ribosomal (r)RNA genes, and 22 transfer (t)RNA genes ( tRNA Leu     and  tRNA Ser   are duplicated), except for  G .  galeus  which contains 23 tRNA genes where  tRNA Thr   is duplicated. The data presented in this paper can assist other researchers in further elucidating the diversification of triakid species and the phylogenetic relationships within Carcharhiniformes (groundsharks) as mitogenomes accumulate in public repositories.
